title: Use of Let's
tagline: Let's learn 'Let's' — the art of making inclusive suggestions in English!
summary: Day 14 is all about 'Let's' — one of the most commonly used patterns in spoken English. 'Let's' = 'Let us' — a suggestion that includes the speaker and listeners both. आज हम सीखेंगे: basic Let's statements, negative form (Let's not), formal version (Let us), common mistakes to avoid, and how Let's differs from Day 13's 'Let him/her/them'. इस topic को master करने से आप office meetings, daily conversations, और formal speeches में बहुत natural sound करेंगे।
hindiSummary: Let's का अर्थ है 'हम करते हैं' — यह speaker को भी include करता है। Formula: Let's + base verb। Negative: Let's not + base verb। Formal: Let us + base verb। आज हम सीखेंगे: daily life में Let's (चलो खाते हैं, चलो जाते हैं), office में Let's (Let's start the meeting, Let's review the data), formal speeches में Let us (Let us build a better tomorrow), और common mistakes (Let's to go ❌, Let's don't ❌)।
whatYouWillLearn: Formula: Let's + base verb for suggestions including the speaker
Negative form: Let's not + base verb (how to refuse/discourage collectively)
Formal form: Let us + base verb (speeches, notices, formal writing)
Common mistakes: Let's to..., Let's don't..., Let we..., Let's + ing form
Contrast with Day 13: Let's (us included) vs Let him/her/them (permission to others)
Office/professional Let's phrases for meetings and presentations
Motivational and rhetorical uses: Let's give it our best shot!
100 vocabulary words for professional and everyday communication

letsCheatSheet.basic: Let's + base verb (suggestion including speaker) | Example: Let's go, Let's eat, Let's start
letsCheatSheet.negative: Let's not + base verb | Example: Let's not waste time, Let's not argue
letsCheatSheet.formal: Let us + base verb | Example: Let us consider, Let us build, Let us commit
letsCheatSheet.question: Shall we + base verb? (question form) | Example: Shall we begin? = Let's begin
letsCheatSheet.wrongForms: ❌ Let's to go | ❌ Let's don't argue | ❌ Let we start | ❌ Let's going | ❌ Lets go (no apostrophe)
letsCheatSheet.contrast: Let's decide = we decide together | Let him decide = he decides (permission to 3rd person)

motivationalQuote: Every great collaboration starts with two words: 'Let's go!' Master Let's today and you'll never struggle to make suggestions in English again.
